column36: I think the coastal areas attract far too many visitors sometimes, who demand manmade tourist infrastructure, that it tips the balance of being detrimental to protecting the wildlife.  They got to nature but not always to enjoy the actual nature themselves, without stuff 'to do' there.  Perhaps with tourism promotion, there should be responsibility to educate respect for wild and blue spaces too? I remember when I was a child, there were always campaigns to 'take your rubbish home', 'follow the countryside code', 'shut gates behind you' etc, but these messages don't seem to be around as much (or get lost in the volume of the info on the internet & social media).  Actually showing children how to play in nature, safely around water (like the forest schools/fire pit model) would provide those skills for next generations to appreciate these environments more?
